tldr: Replace Convert's SaaS A/B and flag platform with FF4J, a self-hosted Apache 2.0 Java feature toggle library with many storage backends and a Spring Boot starter.
why_switch: Drop Convert's monthly subscription in favor of free OSS
Keep flag evaluation entirely inside your JVM services
Choose from 20+ database backends for flag persistence
Built-in audit trail and REST API for compliance reviews
Reduce SaaS lock-in for a runtime-critical dependency
what_to_export: List of feature flags / experiments and their keys
Variation definitions for any A/B tests
Audit history for compliance, if required
Goals or success metrics tied to experiments
export_method: Use Convert's dashboard or API (consult official Convert docs for the current export endpoint) to retrieve experiments, variations, and audience rules. If a bulk export is unavailable, script their REST API or copy definitions manually. Capture which experiments are pure flags vs. A/B tests, since FF4J handles flagging well but is not an experimentation platform on its own.
import_method: Add the ff4j-core dependency and the Spring Boot starter if you use Spring. Configure a FeatureStore backed by your database of choice (JDBC, Redis, MongoDB, etc., per the official FF4J docs). Declare each flag with a string key, attach FlippingStrategy implementations for targeting and percentage rollouts, and expose the web console / REST API for runtime management. Wrap conditional code paths with ff4j.check("flagKey") or annotation-based @Flip.

concept_mapping.note: FF4J flags are identified by string keys
Built-in strategies plus custom Java implementations
FF4J properties hold typed config values

gotchas: FF4J is Java-only; polyglot stacks need a separate solution per language
Experimentation/analytics features are limited compared to Convert's A/B suite
UI feels dated by 2026 standards; budget UX expectations accordingly
Release cadence has been slow recently; pin versions and review CVEs
Secure the admin console and REST API before exposing them

email.subject: Moving feature flags from Convert to FF4J
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving feature flagging from Convert to FF4J so we drop the subscription and keep evaluation inside our JVM stack. Plan: export flags, audiences, and targeting from Convert, then recreate them as FF4J Features with FlippingStrategy rules backed by our existing database. We'll wire the Spring Boot starter for the admin console and REST API. Note: pure A/B experimentation analytics aren't FF4J's strength, so we'll decide separately whether to keep Convert for active experiments or pair FF4J with another analytics tool. Effort estimate: 2-5 days.

Thanks
